question_text: When light hits a mirror, the angle of incidence equals the angle of reflection. Both angles are measured from which line?

options.label: B
options.text: The normal (a line perpendicular to the surface)

options.label: D
options.text: A horizontal line parallel to the ground

explanation: The law of reflection states that angles of incidence and reflection are always measured from the normal, which is an imaginary line perpendicular (at 90°) to the mirror's surface. This is the standard way physicists measure reflection angles.
